commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=74385e7d2d81a631fe9f2ce219767c2b6ba4f18d)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds memory-flush failure counters, lifecycle events, provider-independent force truncation after repeated failures, and focused tests for those paths.

PR surface: Source +233, Tests +209. Total +442 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level bug: current main catches non-abort memory-flush failure without persisting recovery state, and the caller returns the visible flush error before the normal reply. A live provider-crash reproduction is still not supplied.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blocker is approval of the destructive session-truncation default, not a purely mechanical repair.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): add memory flush failure tracking with escalation to force-truncate
target.body:
## Summary

Fix memory flush failure permanently wedging sessions by adding independent failure tracking with bounded retry and provider-independent force-truncate escalation.

When session tokens exceed the `memoryFlush` threshold, OpenClaw spawns an embedded agent turn to produce a handoff summary before truncating history. If this provider call fails (connection closed, 5xx, timeout), the catch block previously only logged and showed a warning — no state was persisted. Since `hasAlreadyFlushedForCurrentCompaction()` still returned `false`, every subsequent message re-triggered the same doomed flush, creating an infinite loop where no user message was ever answered.

## Changes

### `src/config/sessions/types.ts` (+6 lines)

New optional `SessionEntry` fields for independent failure tracking:
- `memoryFlushFailureCount` — consecutive failures since last successful flush
- `memoryFlushLastFailedAt` — timestamp of last failed attempt
- `memoryFlushLastFailureError` — truncated error message (max 200 chars)

### `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts` (+229/-2 lines)

**Failure tracking (catch block, line ~1491):**
- Non-abort failures: increment `memoryFlushFailureCount`, persist timestamp and truncated error
- Abort errors (user cancel, gateway restart): no failure metadata persisted, flush retries next message
- Lifecycle event emitted: `memory_flush_failed` with attempt count and error

**Force-truncate escalation (3 consecutive failures):**
- Provider-independent: mechanically truncates JSONL transcript to last 20 messages
- Prepends system marker: `[Session context was automatically truncated due to repeated memory flush failures...]`
- Resets all failure counters and token estimates
- Uses `writeTextAtomic` for safe file replacement
- Lifecycle event emitted: `memory_flush_force_truncate`
- Visible warning sent to user

**Success path cleanup (line ~1473):**
- Successful flushes now clear all failure tracking fields

### `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts` (+211 lines)

Five new test cases:
1. `increments memoryFlushFailureCount on non-abort flush failure` — verifies counter, timestamp, error, and lifecycle event
2. `does not track failure on abort error` — verifies abort exclusion
3. `clears failure counters on successful flush` — verifies success resets all failure metadata
4. `triggers force-truncate after MAX_FLUSH_FAILURES consecutive failures` — verifies transcript truncation, marker insertion, counter reset, and lifecycle event
5. `next message retries flush after failure` — verifies no retry suppression (unlike v1 approach)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: memoryFlush failure permanently wedges sessions in an infinite flush→fail→flush retry loop, with no escalation path (Issue #85645)
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Linux `aarch64`), local patched checkout at commit `74385e7d`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On `upstream/main` (commit `827ceb55`), the catch block has zero state persistence and zero escalation. The five test cases authored in this PR do not exist on upstream/main:

```
# upstream/main catch block — no failure tracking, no escalation:
  } catch (err) {
    logVerbose(`memory flush run failed: ${String(err)}`);
    const visibleErrorPayload = buildMemoryFlushErrorPayload(err);
    if (visibleErrorPayload) {
      params.onVisibleErrorPayloads?.([visibleErrorPayload]);
    }
  }
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# Docker build
docker build -t openclaw-test -f Dockerfile.test .

# Target tests (39 tests, 5 new)
docker run --rm openclaw-test

# Regression tests (40 tests)
docker run --rm openclaw-test node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
# Target tests:
 ✓  auto-reply  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts (39 tests) 1120ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  39 passed (39)

# Regression tests:
 ✓  auto-reply  src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts (40 tests) 31ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  40 passed (40)
```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  - Failure path: catch block increments `memoryFlushFailureCount` (independent of success marker), emits `memory_flush_failed` lifecycle event, and does NOT suppress retry on next message
  - Escalation path: after 3 consecutive failures, `forceTruncateSession()` mechanically truncates the JSONL to last 20 messages + system marker, resets all counters, emits `memory_flush_force_truncate` lifecycle event — no LLM required
  - Recovery path: successful flush after transient failure clears all failure metadata
  - Force-truncate test verifies: 25 messages → truncated to 20 + marker (22 lines total), old messages dropped, counters reset

- **What was not tested**: Live end-to-end provider crash with a real OpenClaw Gateway instance. The force-truncate path is tested against real JSONL files on disk but with mocked `runWithModelFallback` rejection. Live provider crash would require a provider that crashes on demand.

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts` — 39 tests passed (Docker + local)
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts` — 40 tests passed (Docker + local)
- [x] `git diff --check` — no whitespace issues
- [x] No AI markers in commit, code, or comments

Partially addresses #85645 (implements failure tracking, bounded retry, and provider-independent force-truncate escalation; configurable escalation policy is out of scope for this PR)
target.author: jerry-xin
